executive_brief: CourseHub is a RESTful API platform designed for course management and student enrollment. It employs a JWT-based authentication system and provides distinct workflows for Instructors to manage courses and Students to track learning progress. The architecture focuses on a strict ownership-based access model and automated progress tracking within a structured course-module hierarchy.

architectural_constraints: Course deletion: Rejected if active enrollments exist (409 Conflict)
Enrollment uniqueness: Student/Course pair must be unique
Course visibility: Only published courses accessible to students/public
Instructor isolation: Can only manage/read courses they own
Student isolation: Can only access/update their own enrollments

maturity_assessment: The project is technically cohesive but currently requires REFINEMENT. While the API contracts and entity relationships are well-defined, the lack of high-level business goals and an explicit scope definition creates a strategic gap. Specifically, the absence of a payment processing workflow despite the presence of 'price_cents' indicates a functional disconnect that must be addressed before full implementation.
critical_dependencies: JWT Bearer Token (Mandatory for all protected routes)
Cascade deletion: Modules must be deleted upon successful course deletion
Referential integrity: Enrollment depends strictly on User and Course entities
ActiveEnrollmentConstraint: Blocking gate for course deletion logic
